name: Webvan
summary: Online grocery delivery pioneer that spent hundreds of millions on warehouses ahead of proven demand; filed for bankruptcy in 2001.
lesson: Building out massive fixed infrastructure ahead of proven demand is fatal when capital markets turn.

name: Munchery
summary: San Francisco prepared-meal delivery startup that scaled kitchens across multiple cities before abruptly shutting down in 2019.
lesson: Centralized meal production plus last-mile delivery is a brutally low-margin combination.

name: Sprig
summary: On-demand healthy meal delivery app that made and delivered its own food; shut down after failing to reach profitable scale.
lesson: Owning the entire supply chain from kitchen to doorstep multiplies costs faster than revenue.

name: Zume Pizza
summary: Robotic pizza-making delivery truck startup that raised $375M from SoftBank at a peak valuation near $4B, pivoted to sustainable packaging, then wound down entirely.
lesson: Flashy robotics can't substitute for validated product-market fit before scaling aggressively.

name: Convoy
summary: Digital freight brokerage once valued at $3.8B that shut down after a slowing freight market and a failed sale process.
lesson: Even well-funded marketplaces can't outrun a cyclical downturn without a path to profitability.

name: Juicero
summary: Startup selling a $400 WiFi-connected juice press for proprietary produce packs; collapsed after reporters showed the packs could be squeezed by hand.
lesson: Over-engineering a solution to a problem customers didn't have destroys trust fast.

name: Gorillas
summary: Berlin rapid grocery delivery pioneer that scaled to dozens of cities in under two years before being acquired by rival Getir in a distressed down-round deal.
lesson: Hypergrowth in 10-minute grocery delivery outran the ability to reach profitable dark-store economics.

name: Berkshire Grey
summary: Warehouse robotics and fulfillment automation company that went public via SPAC at a rich valuation, then was taken private by SoftBank at a fraction of that price.
lesson: A SPAC valuation built on future promise can collapse fast once real-world adoption lags.

name: Boxed
summary: Bulk online grocery retailer that went public via SPAC and filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after failing to reach profitability.
lesson: Competing against Amazon and Costco on bulk grocery e-commerce required scale it never reached profitably.

name: Blue Apron
summary: Pioneer of the US meal-kit category that IPO'd near a $2B valuation; taken private by Wonder Group in 2023 for about $103M.
lesson: High customer acquisition costs and low retention plagued meal-kit subscriptions even for the category's pioneer.

name: Grubhub
summary: Early US food delivery leader acquired by Just Eat Takeaway for $7.3B; after years of losses it was sold to Wonder Group in 2024 for roughly $650M.
lesson: First-mover advantage in food delivery eroded fast once DoorDash and Uber Eats out-invested in growth.

name: Getir
summary: Istanbul-founded rapid grocery delivery pioneer that expanded aggressively into the US, UK and Germany, acquiring Gorillas and FreshDirect, then retreated from all three markets in 2023.
lesson: Aggressive international expansion in quick-commerce outpaced the ability to reach profitability anywhere.

name: TuSimple
summary: Autonomous trucking company that IPO'd in 2021 but wound down its US trucking operations in 2024 amid a federal fraud investigation.
lesson: Governance scandals can destroy an AV company's public-market credibility even after a successful IPO.

name: DoorDash
summary: Palo Alto food delivery platform that became the largest US food delivery service by market share and went public in December 2020.
lesson: Focusing early on underserved suburban markets before competitors gave DoorDash durable market share.

name: Instacart
summary: San Francisco grocery delivery marketplace connecting shoppers with retail partners went public in September 2023 as the dominant US grocery delivery platform.
lesson: Partnering with existing grocery retailers rather than building owned warehouses let Instacart scale asset-light.

name: HelloFresh
summary: Berlin-founded meal kit company that scaled globally and went public on the Frankfurt exchange, becoming the world's largest meal kit provider.
lesson: Disciplined marketing spend and international diversification helped HelloFresh outlast most meal-kit competitors.

name: Toast
summary: Boston restaurant point-of-sale and payments platform expanded into a broad restaurant operations suite and went public on the NYSE in 2021.
lesson: Bundling payments, POS hardware and software created strong switching costs and recurring revenue.

name: Zipline
summary: Drone delivery company started by delivering medical supplies in Rwanda and Ghana has expanded to commercial delivery across multiple countries, reaching a valuation over $4B.
lesson: Proving a hardware use case in an underserved market first can build the operational track record needed to expand.

name: Nuro
summary: Autonomous delivery-vehicle startup founded by ex-Google engineers has run driverless grocery pilots with Kroger, Domino's and Walmart.
lesson: Focusing on lower-stakes delivery autonomy rather than passenger robotaxis reduced regulatory risk.

name: Swiggy
summary: Bangalore food delivery and quick-commerce platform became one of India's two dominant food delivery players and went public in November 2024.
lesson: Expanding into quick-commerce grocery alongside its core delivery business diversified Swiggy's revenue ahead of its IPO.

name: Zomato
summary: Delhi restaurant discovery and food delivery platform became India's largest food delivery company; went public in 2021 and later acquired quick-commerce leader Blinkit.
lesson: Diversifying from restaurant reviews into delivery, and later acquiring quick-commerce, let Zomato compound multiple revenue streams.

name: Deliveroo
summary: London food delivery platform went public despite a rocky debut, then was acquired by DoorDash in 2024 for approximately £2.9B.
lesson: Sustained operational focus can make a company an attractive acquisition target despite a disappointing IPO.

name: Wolt
summary: Helsinki food delivery platform known for strong operational execution across Nordic and Central European markets; acquired by DoorDash in 2022 for $8.1B.
lesson: Operational excellence in mid-sized European markets made Wolt a premium acquisition target.

name: Rebel Foods
summary: Mumbai company that pivoted fully into a cloud kitchen model, operating multiple delivery-only restaurant brands from shared kitchens; became the world's largest cloud kitchen operator.
lesson: Pivoting into a multi-brand cloud kitchen model let Rebel Foods scale far more capital-efficiently than traditional chains.
